(1).jpg"},"text":"\u003cdiv class=\"payload-richtext\"\u003e\u003cp\u003eLearn more about contacting the network, ad sales, licensing and our social media channels below. Looking for our app? Go to \u003ca href=\"https://www.bigtenplus.com/\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\"\u003e\n        BigTenPlus.com\n      \u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eFAQ\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Do I have Big Ten Network?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: \u003ca href=\"/btn/channel-finder/\"\u003e\n        Click here to get your channel number for the Big Ten Network\n      \u003c/a\u003e. Big Ten Network is available on all major video providers, such as DIRECTV, DISH, Verizon FiOS, AT\u0026T U-Verse, Charter Communications, Comcast Xfinity, Altice USA, Cox Communications, Mediacom, RCN, WOW!, and approximately 300 additional video providers across North America. Big Ten Network is also available through the majority of OTT providers, including DirecTV Now, YouTube TV, Hulu, Sling TV and fuboTV\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: What channel is Big Ten Network?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: \u003ca href=\"/btn/channel-finder/\"\u003e\n        Our ChannelFinder feature\n      \u003c/a\u003e helps you identify your Big Ten Network channel.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: How many events are on Big Ten Network annually?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Big Ten Network is on the air 24 hours per day, 365 days a year. The network nationally televises more than 550 events and \u003ca href=\"http://bigtenplus.com\"\u003e\n        streams approximately 1,700 more events each\n      \u003c/a\u003e year. Each year's schedule includes approximately 40+ football games, 155+ regular season men's basketball games, Big Ten Men's Basketball Tournament games, 55+ women's basketball games, Big Ten Women's Basketball Tournament games, hundreds of additional Olympic sports events and dozens of Big Ten championship events.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Is there a good balance between men’s and women’s sports on Big Ten Network?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: The network provides comparable coverage of men's and women's sports across all platforms. Big Ten Network covers a variety of women's sports including, basketball, ice hockey, golf, lacrosse, rowing, soccer, softball, swimming and diving, tennis, track and field, and volleyball. Each week during the academic year, the network produces and airs the Big Ten Women's Sports Report, a program dedicated exclusively to the highlights and accomplishments of female Big Ten student-athletes.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: How can I stream Big Ten Network?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Big Ten Network offers two different streaming services, the FOX Sports App and B1G+.The FOX Sports App is a 24/7 simulcast of Big Ten Network that delivers live programming to existing Big Ten Network subscribers via tablets, smartphones, and PCs as well as on-demand access to Big Ten Network's deep archive of games and original programming and Extra Football Game Channels. For more information please visit \u003ca href=\"https://www.foxsports.com/mobile\"\u003e\n        FoxSports.com/Mobile\n      \u003c/a\u003e. B1G+ is the network's subscription-based streaming service that provides coverage of more than 1,700 non-televised Big Ten events. Events include non-conference men's basketball, women's basketball, wrestling, volleyball, and baseball. Football games are not available via B1G+. For more information please visit \u003ca href=\"http://www.bigtenplus.com\"\u003e\n        bigtenplus.com\n      \u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Which football and basketball games air on Big Ten Network?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Each season the Big Ten Conference divides its home games between its television partners, ensuring games are televised to a national audience. Television agreements for games that are contested at away or neutral sites are determined by the host school or organization.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ. Which Olympic sporting events (e.g. field hockey, soccer, wrestling, etc.) air on the network?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: In the fall, Big Ten Network airs wrestling, women's volleyball, men's and women's soccer, field hockey, and men's and women's cross country. In the winter, Big Ten Network airs ice hockey, men's and women's swimming and diving, men's and women's indoor track and field, and men's and women's gymnastics. In the spring, Big Ten Network airs baseball, softball, men's and women's tennis, men’s and women’s golf, men's and women's outdoor track and field, and rowing. The conference and its television partners determine which events will be televised. Consideration is given to appealing matchups while striving to achieve a balance between sports and universities represented. Big Ten Network does include a number of wildcard dates in the schedule to allow some matchup flexibility.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Does Big Ten Network televise multiple football games when there are overlapping games?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Big Ten Network uses Extra Football Game Channels to televise Big Ten Network football games occurring at the same time. These Extra Football Game Channels are only available on Saturdays during the football season. Big Ten Network has offered all of its TV providers the opportunity to make these Extra Football Game Channels available to their subscribers. Satellite providers such as DIRECTV and DISH and telco providers, including AT\u0026T U-Verse and Verizon FiOS, make every Big Ten Network football broadcast available on either the primary Big Ten Network channel or Extra Football Game Channels. Cable systems usually assign different channel numbers in every market for the Extra Football Game Channels. All “Extra Football Game Channels” are also available on the FOX Sports app. Channel numbers typically are assigned mid-week for that weekend's games. Check your channel number for the game you want to watch.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: How are football and basketball start times determined?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Big Ten Network works with the conference, universities, and other television partners to determine the start times for each Big Ten football and basketball game. The time zone of where the game is played and facility availability also factor into the decision. Typically, football games are aired at noon, 3:30 and 8 p.m. ET.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: I missed the game, when will it replay?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Big Ten Network's programming schedule, up to the next 14 days, \u003ca href=\"/btn/shows/\"\u003e\n        is available for viewing here\n      \u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Does Big Ten Network televise NCAA Championship events?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: The NCAA controls the television and digital rights for all NCAA Tournament and Championship events. In some cases, the NCAA will grant the Big Ten Network the rights to broadcast specific games. Over the past several years, Big Ten Network has televised such tournament games as Northwestern women's lacrosse, Michigan softball, and Purdue baseball.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Does Big Ten Network produce games and programming in HD?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Big Ten Network produces and makes available all of its programming and live events in HD. It is the decision of each individual TV provider to offer BTN programming in HD.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Does Big Ten Network offer radio broadcasts for live events?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Each individual school owns the rights to their radio broadcasts, and therefore Big Ten Network cannot use radio broadcasts for Big Ten events.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ. How does Big Ten Network involve students in productions?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA. Since the network launched, Big Ten Network has provided hundreds of students an opportunity to participate in live sports production and internships. In 2009, Big Ten Network launched Student U, in which Big Ten students on each campus produce and announce events, offering them a unique opportunity to gain real-world experience and for the network to train the production stars of tomorrow. The events are streamed live, and many then air on delay on Big Ten Network, providing unprecedented exposure for the conference's Olympic sports programs.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: Where are Big Ten Network studios located?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Our studios and offices are located in the historic Montgomery Ward Catalog Building in Chicago’s River North neighborhood.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ. How do I apply for a job at Big Ten Network?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Any available positions at Big Ten Network will be posted at \u003ca href=\"http://foxcareers.com/#/home\"\u003e\n        FoxCareers.com.\n      \u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eQ: I am interested in advertising with Big Ten Network?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003cbr /\u003eA: Please send a note to \u003ca href=\"mailto:matt.cleary@fox.com?subject=BTN%20website%20Sales%20Inquiry\"\u003e\n        Matt Cleary, Director of Sales at the Big Ten Network\n      \u003c/a\u003e. We look forward to hearing from you.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eContact BTN\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eAttention customers of B1G+: If you're looking to cancel your subscription to B1G+, or need additional assistance, please visit \u003ca href=\"https://support.bigtenplus.com/hc/en-us\"\u003e\n        support.bigtenplus.com\n      \u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eIf it is a football Saturday and you are looking for your game, please use our \u003ca href=\"/btn/game-finder/\"\u003e\n        TV Football GameFinder\n      \u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eIf you are looking for our general TV schedule for the next 14 days, \u003ca href=\"/btn/shows/\"\u003e\n        it's here\n      \u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eIf you are looking for B1G+ customer service, please visit \u003ca href=\"https://support.bigtenplus.com/hc/en-us\"\u003e\n        support.bigtenplus.com\n      \u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eConnect with Big Ten Network Ad Sales\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eInterested in advertising with Big Ten Network? This is because it takes at least 56 days (eight weeks) for your body to replenish the red blood cells you donate. With this requirement in mind, one could donate whole blood a maximum of two times during the “We Give Blood” drive to stay within these parameters. You can, however, donate platelets every seven days. In addition to whole blood donation, platelet donation also counts towards the “We Give Blood” drive and is a great way for donors to rally behind their school of choice and show their school pride multiple times throughout the competition.\u003c/p\u003e","updatedAt":"2025-08-31T18:20:04.990Z","createdAt":"2025-08-20T06:58:31.789Z"},{"id":20,"tenant":{"id":1,"name":"Big Ten","alias":"b1g","updatedAt":"2025-07-09T22:34:21.164Z","createdAt":"2025-07-09T22:34:21.135Z"},"external_id":"blt28059f3162fa77a0","title":"15) How do I get a limited-edition “We Give Blood” drive T-shirt?","type":"Blood","question":"15) How do I get a limited-edition “We Give Blood” drive T-shirt?","answer_rt":"\u003cp\u003eAnyone who presents to donate during the “We Give Blood” drive can claim one of the 18 limited-edition university T-shirts designed by Homefield. Blood donations are needed every two seconds on average for a wide variety of medical reasons, including trauma and accident victims, mothers experiencing complications after childbirth, and individuals receiving cancer treatment. As people get older, they become less likely to be eligible to donate blood. At the same time, the number of young people donating blood has been falling in recent years. \u0026nbsp; With the “We Give Blood” drive competition, Abbott is working with Big Ten universities and blood centers across the country to ignite the next generation of lifelong blood donors.\u003c/p\u003e","updatedAt":"2025-08-31T18:20:04.904Z","createdAt":"2024-09-12T22:15:13.485Z"},{"id":19,"tenant":{"id":1,"name":"Big Ten","alias":"b1g","updatedAt":"2025-07-09T22:34:21.164Z","createdAt":"2025-07-09T22:34:21.135Z"},"external_id":"blt79ae0ceb9bdb595c","title":"17) What happens to my blood after it is collected?","type":"Blood","question":"17) What happens to my blood after it is collected? ","answer_rt":"\u003cp\u003eThe donation is tested for transfusion-transmitted infectious disease to ensure it is safe for patients. These tests cannot diagnose disease; only a healthcare provider can make that determination. We encourage every donor to review the donation center's consent and ask questions about the center's medication deferred list.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eFor more information on what happens to your blood after it’s collected, please visit:\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://www.aabb.org/regulatory-and-advocacy/regulatory-affairs/regulatory-for-blood/donor-safety-screening-and-testing\" target=\"_blank\"\u003ehttps://www.aabb.org/regulatory-and-advocacy/regulatory-affairs/regulatory-for-blood/donor-safety-screening-and-testing\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e","updatedAt":"2025-08-31T18:20:04.831Z","createdAt":"2025-08-20T07:00:14.994Z"},{"id":18,"tenant":{"id":1,"name":"Big Ten","alias":"b1g","updatedAt":"2025-07-09T22:34:21.164Z","createdAt":"2025-07-09T22:34:21.135Z"},"external_id":"blt915538d66b6fa2ec","title":"18) What is the difference between whole blood donation and platelet donation?","type":"Blood","question":"18) What is the difference between whole blood donation and platelet donation?","answer_rt":"\u003cp\u003eWhole blood donation takes about 10 minutes and includes red blood cells, plasma, and platelets, which are later separated for use. It can be done every 56 days. Platelet donation takes 1.5–2.5 hours and collects only platelets while returning the rest of your blood. Platelets are especially needed for cancer and transplant patients and can be donated every 7 days, up to 24 times per year. Both whole blood and platelet donation are accepted in the We Give Blood drive and count as one point toward your school of choice.\u003c/p\u003e","updatedAt":"2025-08-31T18:20:04.778Z","createdAt":"2025-08-20T07:01:01.218Z"},{"id":17,"tenant":{"id":1,"name":"Big Ten","alias":"b1g","updatedAt":"2025-07-09T22:34:21.164Z","createdAt":"2025-07-09T22:34:21.135Z"},"external_id":"blt959b2f51a0513a46","title":"19) I logged my donation/attempt to donate but did not receive my T-shirt email:","type":"Blood","question":"19) I logged my donation/attempt to donate but did not receive my T-shirt email:","answer_rt":"\u003cp\u003eYou should have received an email from Abbott \u0026amp; Big Ten (contact@boostsport.ai) that includes both the website, and code to redeem your limited-edition Homefield designed T-shirt.
